name: Abigail Lindemann
tagline: I take messy data and turn it into something people can actually use.
taglineAlternates: Handed a graph, I'll tell you what it's actually saying.
Give me a graph nobody's made sense of yet — that's the fun part.
heroSubline: Business Analytics & Data Science at Trinity — currently mapping Bexar County and building a database of 1.2 million research papers.

paragraphs: Hey, I'm Abigail. I'm a junior at Trinity studying Business Analytics and Data Science, and most days that means being handed a pile of data nobody's made sense of yet and figuring out what it's actually saying. Lately that's a map of every housing-and-food-access overlap in Bexar County, and a database of 1.2 million research papers comparing Trinity against 93 peer and aspirant institutions. The [[projects]] has the real depth — how they got built, what broke, what I'd change.
about.aiNote: Still building toward machine learning — not there yet, but I use AI constantly, and I'm certified in Anthropic's AI Fluency framework and Claude 101.
about.closing: If any of this overlaps with what you're working on, my contact links are below.

aspirations.heading: Where this is going
aspirations.body: There's something satisfying about being handed a graph and figuring out what it's actually telling you — that part hasn't changed. My working theory on where this goes: as AI takes over more of the analytical grunt work, someone still has to make the final call and keep an eye on what the model is doing. I want to be that person. I'm still exploring the exact lane — data science, analytics, something more AI-adjacent — and I'd rather be honest about that than pretend I've got it mapped out.

personalBits: Plays Women's Ultimate Frisbee at Trinity (hybrid cutter/handler)
